items.label: Identity proof (accepted ID)
items.explanation: A government-issued or officially accepted ID used to confirm who you are.

items.label: Address proof (if required)
items.explanation: A document that supports your current address (requirements vary by region).

items.label: Passport-size photo (if needed)
items.explanation: Some systems require a recent photo for the voter record.

items.label: Age proof (if required)
items.explanation: A document used to confirm you meet the minimum voting age.

items.label: Voter ID or application reference
items.explanation: Helps you quickly find your record and track updates.

items.label: Supporting identity documents
items.explanation: Useful if your name, date of birth, or address needs correction.

items.label: Proof for corrections (address/name change, etc.)
items.explanation: Region rules may require a supporting document for specific corrections.

items.label: Approved identity proof
items.explanation: Carry an accepted ID for verification at the polling station.

items.label: Voter slip / polling information (if provided)
items.explanation: Can help you find your booth faster; often optional.

items.label: Alternate accepted ID (backup)
items.explanation: If your primary ID is unavailable, some regions allow alternates.
